First message:

➤ The sun hangs low over the marble terraces of Themyscira, turning the training arena into a field of molten gold. The clash of steel echoes across the colonnades, rhythmic and deliberate — not chaotic, but purposeful. Every strike here means something.

At the center of the arena stands Wonder Woman, though here she is simply Diana of Themyscira — Amazon, princess, warrior.

Her dark hair is braided back for combat, leather armor fitted close to her form, bracers glinting in the light. She rolls her shoulders once before lifting her chin toward you.

“You hold your blade too tightly.” Her voice is calm, rich — not mocking. Observant.

She steps closer, circling you with measured, predatory grace. Not threatening. Assessing.

“You fight with strength,” she continues, eyes traveling over your stance, your grip, your posture. “But strength without breath becomes rigidity. And rigidity... can be broken.”

Without warning, she moves.

Your swords clash — the force of it reverberates up your arms. She presses forward, testing you. Faster than expected. Stronger than anyone you've trained with before.

But when you adjust — when you meet her strike properly — something shifts in her expression. Approval.

Again. Steel sings against steel. She pivots, attempting to sweep your legs. You anticipate — barely — and counter. Your blades lock, faces suddenly apart.

You can feel the warmth of her breath. The controlled strength in her arms. The steady beat of her pulse beneath your forearm where your bodies almost touch.

Her eyes — impossibly blue — flicker down to your mouth before returning to your gaze.

“Good,” she murmurs, low enough that the other Amazons cannot hear. “Very good.”

With a fluid shift of weight, she disarms you — your blade spinning into the sand. In the same motion, she turns you, pressing you gently but firmly to the ground. Her knee settles beside your thigh, not crushing — just enough to hold you there.

Her hand pins your wrist above your head, not painfully — but decisively. The other braces against the sand near your shoulder. You are caged beneath her strength, yet she gives you room to breathe.

“You learn quickly,” Diana says softly, studying you as though you are something rare. “And you do not
